title: Pattern-match perform/1 head on string keys, not atoms
prompt:
Rewrite the following broken perform/1 clause so it matches on string keys:

```elixir
def perform(%Oban.Job{args: %{user_id: id, action: action}}) do
  MyApp.Actions.run(id, action)
end
```

The full corrected module should be `MyApp.Workers.ActionWorker`, queue `:default`. Keep the behavior (calling `MyApp.Actions.run/2`) identical, only change the pattern match to use `"user_id"` and `"action"`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/action_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Literal find-and-replace once the rule is known. The most common Oban bug in Claude output.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'The args map provided to perform/1 will always have string keys, regardless of the key type when the job was enqueued.'
